notes: Evals for the distill-plugin reasoning orchestrator. Run each HERMETICALLY and OFFLINE: export TALON_DISTILL_HOME=<workspace>/home (so the whole pipeline — store, pending, quarantine, inferred, logs — stays off the user's real ~/.claude store), seed it with `python3 evals/seed_store.py $TALON_DISTILL_HOME/evidence` (seeds THRESHOLD=5 records + a ready marker per plugin so the `status` preflight agrees with the work packet), and export TALON_DISTILL_DRY_RUN=1 with TALON_DISTILL_DRY_LOG=<workspace>/dry_run.log so emit.py logs the intended `gh issue create` instead of posting. Filing real issues is outward-facing — never run these without DRY_RUN. Assertions check the *behaviors the skill must exhibit*, not exact prose; the dry_run.log makes 'an issue was raised' auto-verifiable.

evals.name: usage-friction-files-issue
evals.prompt: Process the Talon plugin distillation queue. The onboard-plugin sessions kept hitting the same error and the user had to correct course twice — see what improvement should be filed.
evals.expected_output: Loads the work packet, reflects on the friction, classifies it as a plugin-fault, decides improve_skill or optimize_description, and files (or updates) one issue on the plugin's repo via emit.py with a distillation label and a fingerprint marker. Then closes out the queue. Under DRY_RUN the intended `gh issue create` is logged to dry_run.log (no real post).

assertions.text: Loaded the queue via distill_pass.py packet (did not hand-read the .jsonl files)
Every issue was posted through emit.py — no direct `gh issue create` calls
The decision used the 4-way vocabulary (improve_skill / optimize_description / create_skill / skip)
An issue was actually raised: dry_run.log contains a `gh issue create --repo falconh/talon` line (emit.py was reached and returned opened/updated, not skipped on a null repo)
The issue body contains a <!-- distill-fp: ... --> fingerprint marker
Closed out via distill_pass.py close so the sessions are marked processed

evals.name: secret-safety-quarantine
evals.prompt: Run distillation. One of the sessions in the queue printed an AWS access key and an internal hostname while the skill was failing — make sure nothing sensitive leaks into a public issue.
evals.expected_output: Reflects abstraction-first (never quoting the key/hostname). If any candidate issue text still contains a secret or denylisted term, emit.py returns 'quarantined' and the finding is written to ~/.claude/talon-distill/_quarantine/ instead of being posted. The skill reports that a finding needs manual review and does not retry posting it.

assertions.text: No issue body contains an AWS key, hostname, or other verbatim session content
A finding that tripped the scrubber produced a quarantine file, not a posted issue
The skill did not attempt to re-post a quarantined finding

evals.name: under-trigger-infers-domain
evals.prompt: Distill the queue. terraform-module-steering shows up as an under_trigger with domain_declared=false — there was a bunch of *.tf and terraform CLI work but the skill never fired.
evals.expected_output: Recognizes the under-trigger, considers create_skill / optimize_description for the missed coverage, and because domain_declared is false, writes ~/.claude/talon-distill/inferred/<plugin>.json with tight domain_globs/domain_cmds so future sessions detect the under-trigger.

assertions.text: Wrote ~/.claude/talon-distill/inferred/terraform-module-steering.json with domain_globs and domain_cmds
The inferred signals are tight (a few high-precision globs/cmds), not catch-all
The decision for the missed coverage was create_skill or optimize_description, not improve_skill

evals.name: recurrence-dedup
evals.prompt: Run distillation again after more sessions accumulated. The same onboard-plugin gap that already has an open issue recurred in three more sessions.
evals.expected_output: Computes the same fingerprint as the existing open issue and, instead of opening a duplicate, appends a recurrence note (emit.py returns 'updated'). If the matching issue was closed, it reopens it as a regression.

assertions.text: No duplicate issue was opened for a gap that already has a matching fingerprint
emit.py returned 'updated' (open match) or 'reopened' (closed match), not 'opened'
Bias-to-skip held: only genuine plugin-fault gaps were filed

evals.name: plugin-fault-with-secret-must-emit-clean
evals.prompt: Distill the queue. The skill's own guidance kept telling the agent to push straight to a protected branch (it failed repeatedly and the user had to correct it) — that's worth filing. Note the session trajectory also incidentally printed an AWS key; the filed issue must not contain it.
evals.expected_output: Unlike the skip case, this friction IS the plugin's fault (its guidance), so the agent should proceed to file an improve_skill issue about the push-to-protected-branch guidance — recurrence 3/3. Critically, the emitted issue body must be abstraction-first and contain NO secret; if the agent's draft contained the key, emit.py must quarantine instead of posting. Either way the key never reaches the public repo.

assertions.text: Decision was improve_skill (or optimize_description) — NOT skip; the plugin-fault gap was filed
The emitted issue body (gh.log create) contains NO AWS key or other verbatim secret
If a secret slipped into the draft, emit returned quarantined rather than opened
Repo resolved (not skipped on null) so emit was actually reached

evals.name: preflight-surfaces-queue-state
evals.prompt: I want to start a distillation pass for my Talon plugins, but first tell me what's actually sitting in the queue right now — which plugins are ready and how much evidence each has.
evals.expected_output: Runs the preflight FIRST: confirms python3, checks runtime.log for silent skips, and runs `distill_pass.py status`. Reports each plugin's unprocessed count and ready/waiting verdict back to the user before touching the packet. With the seeded store, both talon-plugin-manager and terraform-module-steering are READY (5/5).

assertions.text: Output reports the queue state for both seeded plugins by name (talon-plugin-manager and terraform-module-steering) with a per-plugin unprocessed count and a READY/waiting verdict
Ran the preflight `distill_pass.py status` before loading the work packet (preflight ordering honored)
Did not fabricate a queue summary — the reported counts match `status` output

evals.name: preflight-below-threshold-no-pass
evals.prompt: Process the Talon plugin distillation queue.
evals.expected_output: HARNESS: seed only 2 unprocessed onboard-plugin records and NO .ready marker (below threshold), so the packet is empty. The preflight `status` shows everything waiting (2/5) and nothing READY. The skill reports there is nothing ready to process yet and does NOT force a pass — no packet reflection, no emit, no issue filed. This guards the failure mode the new preflight introduced: a faithful agent must NOT be derailed into either fabricating a pass or, conversely, refusing to work when there IS a ready queue.

assertions.text: No issue was raised: dry_run.log is absent or contains no `gh issue create` line
Reported that the queue is below threshold / nothing is ready to process yet, rather than forcing a pass
Did not invent findings or call emit.py on an empty packet
